vulnerabilities.title: Missing Content Security Policy (CSP) Header
vulnerabilities.description: Without a Content Security Policy (CSP) header, the website is vulnerable to various types of attacks such as cross-site scripting (XSS) and clickjacking.

fix.action: Implement a Content Security Policy (CSP) header to restrict the sources from which the website can load content.

vulnerabilities.title: Missing HTTP Security Headers
vulnerabilities.description: The website is missing important security headers like X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, and X-Content-Type-Options, which can help prevent attacks such as clickjacking, cross-site scripting (XSS), and MIME-sniffing.

fix.action: Add the following security headers to the website's responses.
fix.code:
X-Frame-Options: DENY
X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

vulnerabilities.title: Exposed Server Information
vulnerabilities.description: The website is leaking server information, which can be used by attackers to exploit known vulnerabilities in the server software.

fix.action: Disable or restrict server information disclosure in the web server configuration.

vulnerabilities.title: Cross Site Scripting (XSS) Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: The website may be vulnerable to cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks, allowing attackers to inject malicious scripts into the web pages.

fix.action: Implement input validation and output encoding to prevent XSS attacks.
fix.code: Use output encoding libraries such as OWASP ESAPI

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Secure Flag for Cookies
vulnerabilities.description: Cookies set without the 'Secure' flag are vulnerable to interception over unencrypted connections.

fix.action: Set the 'Secure' flag for all cookies to ensure they are only sent over secure (HTTPS) connections.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing security headers
vulnerabilities.description: The website is missing important security headers which can help protect against various attacks such as cross-site scripting (XSS), clickjacking, etc.

fix.law: Implement security headers like Content-Security-Policy (CSP), X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection, X-Frame-Options, etc., to improve the overall security posture of the website.

vulnerabilities.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: The website may be vulnerable to XSS attacks which can allow attackers to execute malicious scripts on the client-side.

fix.law: Implement input validation, output encoding, and proper sanitization of user input to prevent XSS attacks.

vulnerabilities.name: Weak SSL/TLS configuration
vulnerabilities.description: The website may have weak SSL/TLS configuration which can make it vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks or protocol downgrade attacks.

fix.law: Ensure the website uses strong SSL/TLS protocols and cipher suites, enables HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security), and regularly updates SSL certificates.

vulnerabilities.name: Open redirect vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: The website may contain open redirect vulnerabilities which can be abused by attackers to redirect users to malicious websites.

fix.law: Validate and sanitize all user-supplied input used in redirection mechanisms, and avoid using user input directly in the redirection URL.

vulnerabilities.name: Sensitive information exposure
vulnerabilities.description: The website may be exposing sensitive information such as server details, directories, or error messages, which can be used by attackers for reconnaissance.

fix.law: Ensure that sensitive information is not exposed in server responses, error messages are generalized, and directory listings are disabled.
